questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText:
Let the function $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be defined by

$$ f(x)=\frac{\sin x}{e^{\pi x}} \frac{\left(x^{2023}+2024 x+2025\right)}{\left(x^2-x+3\right)}+\frac{2}{e^{\pi x}} \frac{\left(x^{2023}+2024 x+2025\right)}{\left(x^2-x+3\right)} . $$

Then the number of solutions of $f(x)=0$ in $\mathbb{R}$ is _________.
questions.solution: $$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{\left(\mathrm{x}^{2023}+2024 \mathrm{x}+2025\right)}{\mathrm{e}^{\pi \mathrm{x}}\left(\mathrm{x}^2-\mathrm{x}+3\right)}(\sin \mathrm{x}+2) \\ & \because(\sin \mathrm{x}+2) \text { is never zero } \\ & \therefore \text { for } \mathrm{x}^{2223}+2024 \mathrm{x}+2025=0 \\ & \text { let } \phi(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{x}^{2023}+2024 \mathrm{x}+2025 \\ & \phi^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=2023 \mathrm{x}^{2022}+2024>0 \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R} \\ & \therefore \phi(\mathrm{x}) \text { is an Strictly Increasing function } \\ & \therefore \phi(\mathrm{x})=0 \text { for exactly one value of } \mathrm{x} \\ & \therefore \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=0 \text { has one solution } \end{aligned}$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$ f(x)=\frac{x}{1-x}, \qquad x>0,\ x\ne 1 $

We have to find

$ (1-x)f(\sqrt{x})+x f(\sqrt{x}+1) $

Let $t=\sqrt{x}$. Then $x=t^2$.

Now,

$ f(\sqrt{x})=f(t)=\frac{t}{1-t} $

and

$ f(\sqrt{x}+1)=f(t+1)=\frac{t+1}{1-(t+1)}=\frac{t+1}{-t}=-\frac{t+1}{t} $

Substitute these into the expression:

$ (1-x)f(\sqrt{x})+x f(\sqrt{x}+1) $

$ =(1-t^2)\cdot \frac{t}{1-t}+t^2\left(-\frac{t+1}{t}\right) $

Now simplify step by step.

Since

$ 1-t^2=(1-t)(1+t), $

so

$ (1-t^2)\cdot \frac{t}{1-t}=(1+t)t $

Also,

$ t^2\left(-\frac{t+1}{t}\right)=-t(t+1) $

Therefore,

$ (1+t)t - t(t+1)=0 $

Hence,

$ (1-x)f(\sqrt{x})+x f(\sqrt{x}+1)=0 $

So the correct option is:

$ \boxed{0} $

Option 4.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is a possible expression for g(x)?
questions.solution:
To determine a possible expression for $ g(x) $, start by considering the compositions of $ g $ and $ f $.

Given $ f(x) = x^2 - 1 $ and $ (g \circ f)(x) = x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $:

$$ g(f(x)) = g(x^2 - 1) = x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $$

We want to find $ g(x) $ such that $ g(x^2 - 1) = x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $. Let's check each option:

Option A: $ \sqrt{x+1}-\sqrt[4]{x+1} $

$$ g(x^2 - 1) = \sqrt{(x^2-1)+1} - \sqrt[4]{(x^2-1)+1} = \sqrt{x^2} - \sqrt[4]{x^2} = x - x^{1/2} $$

This does not match $ x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $.

Option B: $ \sqrt{x+1}-\sqrt[4]{x+1}+1 $

$$ g(x^2 - 1) = \sqrt{(x^2-1)+1} - \sqrt[4]{(x^2-1)+1} + 1 = \sqrt{x^2} - \sqrt[4]{x^2} + 1 = x - x^{1/2} + 1 $$

This matches $ x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $.

Option C: $ \sqrt{x+1}+\sqrt[4]{x+1} $

$$ g(x^2 - 1) = \sqrt{(x^2-1)+1} + \sqrt[4]{(x^2-1)+1} = \sqrt{x^2} + \sqrt[4]{x^2} = x + x^{1/2} $$

This does not match $ x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $.

Option D: $ x+1-\sqrt{x+1}+1 $

$$ g(x^2 - 1) = (x^2 - 1) + 1 - \sqrt{(x^2-1)+1} + 1 = x^2 - \sqrt{x^2} + 1 = x^2 - x + 1 $$

This does not match $ x - \sqrt{x} + 1 $.

The possible expression for $ g(x) $ is:

$$ \sqrt{x+1} - \sqrt[4]{x+1} + 1 $$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Let $f(x)=\frac{2^{x+2}+16}{2^{2 x+1}+2^{x+4}+32}$. Then the value of $8\left(f\left(\frac{1}{15}\right)+f\left(\frac{2}{15}\right)+\ldots+f\left(\frac{59}{15}\right)\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\frac{42^x+16}{2.2^{2 x}+16.2^x+32} \\ & f(x)=\frac{2\left(2^x+4\right)}{2^{2 x}+8.2^x+16} \\ & f(x)=\frac{2}{2^x+4} \\ & f(4-x)=\frac{2^x}{2\left(2^x+4\right)} \\ & f(x)+f(4-x)=\frac{1}{2} \end{aligned}$$

So, $\quad \mathrm{f}\left(\frac{1}{15}\right)+\mathrm{f}\left(\frac{59}{15}\right)=\frac{1}{2}$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { Similarly }=f\left(\frac{29}{15}\right)+f\left(\frac{31}{15}\right)=\frac{1}{2} \\ & f\left(\frac{30}{15}\right)=f(2)=\frac{2}{2^2+4}=\frac{2}{8}=\frac{1}{4} \\ & \Rightarrow 8\left(29 \times \frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}\right) \end{aligned}$$

Ans. 118

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{A}=\{1,2,3, \ldots, 7\}$$ and let $$\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{A})$$ denote the power set of $$\mathrm{A}$$. If the number of functions $$f: \mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{A})$$ such that $$\mathrm{a} \in f(\mathrm{a}), \forall \mathrm{a} \in \mathrm{A}$$ is $$\mathrm{m}^{\mathrm{n}}, \mathrm{m}$$ and $$\mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{N}$$ and $$\mathrm{m}$$ is least, then $$\mathrm{m}+\mathrm{n}$$ is equal to _________.
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & f: A \rightarrow P(A) \\ & a \in f(a) \end{aligned}$$

That means '$$a$$' will connect with subset which contain element '$$a$$'.

Total options for 1 will be $$2^6$$. (Because $$2^6$$ subsets contains 1)

Similarly, for every other element

Hence, total is $$2^6 \times 2^6 \times 2^6 \times 2^6 \times 2^6 \times 2^6 \times 2^6=2^{42}$$

Ans. $$2+42=44$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Let $f$ be a function such that $f(x)+3 f\left(\frac{24}{x}\right)=4 x, x \neq 0$. Then $f(3)+f(8)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & f(x)+3 f\left(\frac{24}{x}\right)=4 x, x \neq 0 \quad \ldots(1)\\ & \text { replace } x \text { by } \frac{24}{x} \\ & f\left(\frac{24}{x}\right)+3 f\left(\frac{24}{24}\right)=4\left(\frac{24}{x}\right)=\frac{96}{x} \quad \ldots(2) \\ & 3 \times(2)-(1) \\ & \Rightarrow 8 f(x)=\frac{96.3}{x}-4 x \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{36}{x}-\frac{x}{2} \\ & f(3)+f(8)=\left(12-\frac{3}{2}\right)+\left(\frac{36}{8}-4\right) \end{aligned}$$

$=8+\frac{36}{8}-\frac{12}{8}=11$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText:
The range of the function,

$$f(x) = {\log _{\sqrt 5 }}\left( {3 + \cos \left( {{{3\pi } \over 4} + x} \right) + \cos \left( {{\pi \over 4} + x} \right) + \cos \left( {{\pi \over 4} - x} \right) - \cos \left( {{{3\pi } \over 4} - x} \right)} \right)$$ is :
questions.solution:
$$f(x) = {\log _{\sqrt 5 }}\left( {3 + \cos \left( {{{3\pi } \over 4} + x} \right) + \cos \left( {{\pi \over 4} + x} \right) + \cos \left( {{\pi \over 4} - x} \right) - \cos \left( {{{3\pi } \over 4} - x} \right)} \right)$$

$$f(x) = {\log _{\sqrt 5 }}\left[ {3 + 2\cos \left( {{\pi \over 4}} \right)\cos (x) - 2\sin \left( {{{3\pi } \over 4}} \right)\sin (x)} \right]$$

$$f(x) = {\log _{\sqrt 5 }}\left[ {3 + \sqrt 2 (\cos x - \sin x)} \right]$$

Since $$ - \sqrt 2 \le \cos x - \sin x \le \sqrt 2 $$

$$ \Rightarrow {\log _{\sqrt 5 }}\left[ {3 + \sqrt 2 \left( { - \sqrt 2 } \right) \le f(x) \le {{\log }_{\sqrt 5 }}\left[ {3 + \sqrt 2 \left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)} \right]} \right]$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\log _{\sqrt 5 }}(1) \le f(x) \le {\log _{\sqrt 5 }}(5)$$

So, Range of f(x) is [0, 2]

Option (d)

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:

1. $f(x)$ is differentiable for all $x < 0$

2. $g(x)$ is continuous at $x = 0.0001$

3. The derivative of $g(x)$ at $x = 2.5$ is 1

Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.solution:
Let $ f(x) = |x| + 1 $ and $ g(x) = [x] - 1 $, where $[x]$ denotes the greatest integer function. Define $ h(x) = \frac{f(x)}{g(x)} $.

Analysis of the statements:

$f(x)$ is differentiable for all $ x < 0 $:

$ f(x) = |x| + 1 $

For $ x < 0 $, $ |x| = -x $, thus $ f(x) = -x + 1 $

This is a linear function, and hence differentiable for all $ x < 0 $.

$g(x)$ is continuous at $ x = 0.0001 $:

$ g(x) = [x] - 1 $

For $ 0 < x < 1 $, $[x] = 0$

Therefore, $ g(x) = [0.0001] - 1 = 0 - 1 = -1 $

The greatest integer function $[x]$ is continuous at non-integer points, including $ x = 0.0001 $.

The derivative of $g(x)$ at $ x = 2.5 $ is 1:

$ g(x) = [x] - 1 $

The function $[x]$ has derivative 0 everywhere except at the integers, where it is not differentiable.

At $ x = 2.5 $, $[x] = 2$

The derivative of $ g(x) $ at $ x = 2.5 $ is 0 (as it inherits the properties of the greatest integer function).

Correct Statements:

The correct statements are 1 and 2 only. Therefore, the answer is:

Option A

1 and 2 only

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Let $$f: \mathbf{R}-\left\{\frac{-1}{2}\right\} \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$$ and $$g: \mathbf{R}-\left\{\frac{-5}{2}\right\} \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$$ be defined as $$f(x)=\frac{2 x+3}{2 x+1}$$ and $$g(x)=\frac{|x|+1}{2 x+5}$$. Then, the domain of the function fog is :
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\frac{2 x+3}{2 x+1} ; x \neq-\frac{1}{2} \\ & g(x)=\frac{|x|+1}{2 x+5}, x \neq-\frac{5}{2} \end{aligned}$$

Domain of $$f(g(x))$$

$$f(g(x))=\frac{2 g(x)+3}{2 g(x)+1}$$

$$x \neq-\frac{5}{2}$$ and $$\frac{|x|+1}{2 x+5} \neq-\frac{1}{2}$$

$$x \in R-\left\{-\frac{5}{2}\right\}$$ and $$x \in R$$

$$\therefore$$ Domain will be $$\mathrm{R}-\left\{-\frac{5}{2}\right\}$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Let f(x) = 2<sup>10</sup>.x + 1 and g(x)=3<sup>10</sup>.x $$-$$ 1. If (fog) (x) = x,  then x is equal to :
questions.solution:
(fog) (x)   =   x

$$ \Rightarrow $$$$\,\,\,$$ f (g(x))   =   x

$$ \Rightarrow $$$$\,\,\,$$ f (310. x $$-$$ 1)   =   x    [ as    g(x) = 310. x $$-$$ 1]

$$ \Rightarrow $$$$\,\,\,$$ 210 . (310 . x $$-$$ 1) + 1   =   x

$$ \Rightarrow $$$$\,\,\,$$ 310 . x $$-$$ 1 + 2$$-$$10   =   x . 2$$-$$10   [dividing by 210]

$$ \Rightarrow $$$$\,\,\,$$310 . x $$-$$ 2$$-$$10 . x   =   1 $$-$$ 2$$-$$10

$$ \Rightarrow $$$$\,\,\,$$ x (310 $$-$$ 2$$-$$ 10)   =   1$$-$$ 2$$-$$10

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$\,\,\,$$ x = $${{1 - {2^{ - 10}}} \over {{3^{10}} - {2^{ - 10}}}}$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Let a function f : (0, $$\infty $$) $$ \to $$ (0, $$\infty $$) be defined by f(x) = $$\left| {1 - {1 \over x}} \right|$$. Then f is :
options: not injective but it is surjective
both injective as well as surjective
questions.solution:
$$f\left( x \right) = \left| {1 - {1 \over x}} \right| = {{\left| {x - 1} \right|} \over x} = \left\{ {\matrix{ {{{1 - x} \over x}} & {0 < x \le 1} \cr {{{x - 1} \over x}} & {x \ge 1} \cr } } \right.$$


$$ \Rightarrow $$  f(x) is not injective

but range of function is $$\left[ {0,\infty } \right)$$

Remarks :  If co-domain is $$\left[ {0,\infty } \right)$$, then f(x) will be surjective.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: If the function $$f(x) = {x^3} + {e^{x/2}}$$ and $$g(x) = {f^{ - 1}}(x)$$, then the value of $$g'(1)$$ is _________.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText:
Let $$a$$, b, c $$ \in R$$. If $$f$$(x) = ax2 + bx + c is such that
$$a$$ + b + c = 3 and $$f$$(x + y) = $$f$$(x) + $$f$$(y) + xy, $$\forall x,y \in R,$$

then $$\sum\limits_{n = 1}^{10} {f(n)} $$ is equal to
questions.solution:
f(x) = ax2 + bx + c

f(1) = a + b + c = 3 $$ \Rightarrow $$ f (1) = 3

Now f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y) + xy ...(1)

Put x = y = 1 in eqn (1)

f(2) = f(1) + f(1) + 1

= 2f(1) + 1

$$ \Rightarrow $$ f(2) = 7

Similarly f(3) = 12

f(4) = 18

$$\sum\limits_{n = 1}^{10} {f(n)} $$ = 3 + 7 + 12 + 18 + 25 + 33 + 42 + 52 + 63 + 75 = 330

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText:
Let $$f, g: \mathbf{R} \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$$ be defined as :

$$f(x)=|x-1| \text { and } g(x)= \begin{cases}\mathrm{e}^x, & x \geq 0 \\ x+1, & x \leq 0 .\end{cases}$$

Then the function $$f(g(x))$$ is
options:
neither one-one nor onto.
one-one but not onto.
both one-one and onto.
onto but not one-one.
questions.solution:
$$\begin{aligned} & f(x)= \begin{cases}x-1, & x \geq 1 \\ 1-x & x<0\end{cases} \\ & g(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc} e^x & ; \quad x \geq 0 \\ x+1 & ; \quad x \leq 0 \end{array}\right. \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} f(g(x)) & = \begin{cases}g(x)-1, & g(x) \geq 1 \\ 1-g(x) & g(x)<1\end{cases} \\ & =\left\{\begin{array}{cl} e^x-1 & x \geq 0 \\ -x & x<0 \end{array}\right. \end{aligned}$$
